findings.title: Parent backend specs still describe process_manager fallback to current_backend_registry
findings.situation_simple: The updated process_manager spec and code require the caller to pass backend explicitly, but parent/current-backend specs still say process_manager chooses a backend from current_backend_registry when session_id is None or backend is absent.
findings.implementation_risk: A future implementer can reintroduce the rejected implicit fallback path, add a failing test around send_message(session_id=None), or make new sessions bypass the bot/session_manager ownership contract.
evidence: dev/docs/specs/realised/coding_agent_backend_spec.md:614-729 says current_backend_registry.get_current() is used when session_id is None or backend is not passed
dev/docs/specs/realised/current_backend_registry_spec.md:35-38 says process_manager calls current_backend_registry.get_current() to choose the backend for subprocess launch
dev/docs/specs/realised/08.05_16.38-backend-aware-process_manager_spec.md:25 states backend-aware send_message requires explicit backend and backend=None is a contract error
src/claude_manager/process_manager.py:780-790 rejects backend=None, and tests/test_process_manager.py:372-375 verifies this behavior

options.label: Update parent/current specs
options.effect: Make coding_agent_backend_spec and current_backend_registry_spec say bot/session_manager choose the current backend for new sessions, then pass an explicit backend and temp session_id into process_manager.

options.label: Revert process_manager contract
options.effect: Reintroduce implicit fallback from process_manager to current_backend_registry, conflicting with the accepted recommendation and current tests.

findings.recommendation_reason: This preserves the accepted explicit-backend architecture and aligns the realised docs with code and tests.

findings.title: Operational session scans are documented with the recent-list API instead of list_all
findings.situation_simple: Several specs still tell watcher and project pending delivery to use list_session_files_for_project, but the implemented operational path uses list_all_session_files_for_project so it is not capped by the UI recent-session limit.
findings.implementation_risk: Following the specs literally can make watcher or pending delivery miss sessions outside the 15-item UI list or Codex lookback/recent window, causing lost unread messages after project switches or incomplete monitoring.
evidence: dev/docs/specs/realised/08.05_16.38-backend-aware-session_watcher_spec.md:354 and :399 use backend.list_session_files_for_project for watcher polling/resume paths
dev/docs/specs/realised/coding_agent_backend_spec.md:909 defines MAX_RECENT_SESSIONS = 15 as the cap for list_session_files_for_project
src/claude_manager/coding_agent_backend.py:167-172 defines list_all_session_files_for_project as the operational scan method
tests/test_codex_backend.py:397-424 verifies list_all_session_files_for_project ignores recent/lookback limits while list_session_files_for_project is capped

options.label: Document both scan APIs
options.effect: Use list_session_files_for_project only for /sessions UI and list_all_session_files_for_project for watcher, pending delivery, reset, and other operational flows.

options.label: Use recent API everywhere
options.effect: Simplifies the text but accepts data loss for sessions outside the recent UI window.

findings.recommendation_reason: It matches the code, tests, and data-safety requirement that operational flows must not be capped by a UI limit.

findings.title: process_manager_spec still requires an obsolete claude_runner contract
findings.situation_simple: The integration spec correctly allows legacy ClaudeProcess/start_process as compatibility debt, but process_manager_spec still says backend-aware start_process(args, cwd, stdin_bytes) exists and ClaudeProcess.terminate is removed.
findings.implementation_risk: A future change based on process_manager_spec can delete legacy runner behavior that current tests still cover, or rewrite process_manager against a non-existent start_process(args, ...) API instead of start_subprocess_for_backend.
evidence: dev/docs/specs/realised/08.05_16.38-backend-aware-process_manager_spec.md:573 says claude_runner exposes start_process(args, cwd, stdin_bytes) and ClaudeProcess.terminate is removed

dev/docs/specs/realised/telegram_agent_backend_integration_spec.md:349-358 says legacy ClaudeProcess/start_process may remain as compatibility debt and the new backend-aware path is start_subprocess_for_backend
src/claude_manager/claude_runner.py:279-323 implements start_subprocess_for_backend and keeps legacy start_process(session_id, cwd)

options.label: Align process_manager_spec with integration spec
options.effect: Document start_subprocess_for_backend as the new path and mark ClaudeProcess/start_process/terminate as allowed legacy compatibility debt.

options.label: Remove legacy runner path
options.effect: Would require code and test changes outside the accepted text-only fixes and contradict the integration spec.

findings.recommendation_reason: It keeps the accepted compatibility boundary and prevents accidental deletion of tested legacy behavior.
